question.text: A European style option can be exercised:

answer.text: At any time before or on the expiration date
On specific pre-determined dates before expiry
Only during the last week before expiration

explanation.text: **The Logic:** European options can only be exercised on the expiration date, not before. This is the defining characteristic. **The Trap:** 'At any time' describes American options. 'Specific pre-determined dates' describes Bermudan options. **Cross-Exam Context:** Exchange-traded interest rate options in India are European style -- this is a regulatory fact tested in NISM Series IV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** European options are generally easier to price using Black-Scholes because there is no early exercise possibility to account for.
